person.name: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex

nameForms.fullText: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex

km.Warwickshire, England: វ៉ារវីកស៊ីរe, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex於1465年出生在英國瓦維其可爾。他和Virginia Cox至少有1個女兒。他去世於1525年在他的家鄉享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex于1465年出生在英国瓦維其可爾。他和Virginia Cox至少有1个女儿。他去世于1525年在他的家乡享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Grant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish, English, and especially Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname from Anglo-Norman French graund, graunt ‘tall, large’ (Old French grand, grant, from Latin grandis), given either to a person of remarkable size, or else in a relative way to distinguish two bearers of the same personal name, often representatives of different generations within the same family.
English: from the rare Middle English (and Old English) personal name Grante or Grente.
Irish: in Ireland this is usually the Norman Scottish name (see 1 above), but it was also adopted for Irish Mag Raighne, see Graney .

variants.name: Graney
meanings: Irish (Galway, Cork, and Kerry): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mag Raighne ‘son of Raighne’, a pet form of Raghnall. It has sometimes been changed to Grant . See also Rainey .
Possibly an altered form of Swiss German Gräni, unexplained.

variants.name: Grand
meanings: French: nickname from Old French grand, grant ‘tall, large’, from Latin grandis. See also Grant 1 and 4, compare Degrand and Legrand .
German: topographic name for someone who lived in a gravelly area, from Middle Low German grand ‘coarse gravel’.
German: nickname from a southern word of uncertain origin meaning ‘frustration, anger, ire’.

variants.name: Grande
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Rocco, Domenic, Mario, Antonio, Santo, Orlando, Americo, Carmine, Gennaro, Luigi, Pasquale, Saverio, Alberico.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Alicia, Ricardo, Jorge, Juan, Raul, Angel, Atilio.
Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese: nickname for someone of large stature, in either a literal or figurative sense, from grande ‘tall, large’.
German: habitational name from Grande in Holstein or Grand in Bavaria.

variants.name: General
meanings: African American: from the English vocabulary word general, possibly adopted as a surname in honor of a certain general of the US army, most probably Ulysses S. Grant (see Grant , compare President ). Compare also 3 below.
German: nickname for a mercenary (employed in royal services).
Hispanic (mainly Philippines): nickname from Spanish general ‘general’ (military officer).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

en.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex was born in 1465, in Warwickshire, England. He had at least 1 daughter with Virginia Cox. He died in 1525, in his hometown, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex est né en 1465, à Warwickshire, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Virginia Cox. Il est décédé en 1525, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex wurde 1465 in Warwickshire, England geboren. Er hatte mit Virginia Cox mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1525 im Alter von 60 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex è nato il nel 1465 nel Warwickshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Virginia Cox. Morì nel 1525, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.Warwickshire, England: イギリスワーウィクシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essexは1465年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアで生まれた。彼はVirginia Coxとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1525年に故郷で60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex(은)는 1465년 영국 워윅실르에서 태어났다. 그는 Virginia Cox(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1525년 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex nasceu em 1465, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Virginia Cox. Ele faleceu em 1525, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex родился в 1465 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Virginia Cox. Он умер в 1525 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Grant latterly of Stanford Rivers, Essex nació en 1465, en Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Virginia Cox. Murió en 1525, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años.

th.Warwickshire, England: ว่ารวิกชิเร, เอิงแลนด์
